title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
ULIP for Health Benefits: A Simple Way to Plan for Your Future Medical Needs
Many people in India worry about the cost of medical treatment. Even if you have health insurance, some treatments like cancer or heart surgeries might not be fully covered. One way to stay prepared is by planning with a ULIP. Most people search for Health insurance ULIP however, ULIP does not provide health insurance, it provides Life Insurance and money growth. It may help you buy life cover and also build money to manage future health costs.
A ULIP is a smart option if you want both protection and savings in one policy. ULIP is a Life Insurance product, so it's important not to assume that ULIP health benefits exist. Let’s understand more about ULIPs and how they can contribute to your health costs.
Types of ULIP Plans Offered
There are no Health insurance ULIPs however, you can still use them to plan for future medical needs. While ULIPs mainly offer life cover and investment options, you can use the fund value built over time to support your health-related expenses. ULIPs focus more on long-term wealth creation, while some may offer optional riders for critical illness or accidental cover.
You can choose from different ULIP options based on your risk level and goals. For example, if you prefer safety, you can pick plans that invest more in debt funds. If you’re okay with higher risk for better returns, there are equity-focused ULIPs. Some ULIP plans also give the flexibility to switch between funds as your needs change. These features help you stay financially prepared, including for health needs that may come up in the future.
How Can ULIPs Be Used for Health Benefits?
A ULIP gives your life cover and helps you save money for the future. You can use this saved money for your medical needs. Every time you pay your premium, one part goes towards life insurance, and the other part is used to grow your money in funds like equity or debt. Over the years, this accumulated fund can be used for health benefits. Additionally, you can opt to take health-related riders like critical illness benefits can help to get some financial relief when the insured is diagnosed with critical illnesses.
If you or your family face a medical emergency in the future, you can use this money. After a 5-year lock-in period, ULIPs allow partial withdrawals. So, you don’t have to break the full policy—you can just take out what is needed.
You can also choose or switch funds based on your risk-taking capacity. If the market is going up, you may earn more. If it is going down, you can switch to safer funds. Some ULIPs also allow you to add more money at any time, which helps grow your health fund even faster.
So, ULIPs don’t directly pay hospital bills like health insurance, but they help you build a money backup that can be used for big medical expenses anytime in the future.
ULIPs features That Help with Future Medical Needs
ULIPs offer many useful features. They help you protect your family and also grow your money for health and other goals. Here are the main features and benefits:
Life Insurance Cover: In the event of the insured’s death, the insured’s family gets a death benefit and a fixed amount at maturity so that the family can manage their expenses like medical bills or daily expenses. You can also opt for health riders like critical illness rider to get treatment costs of a critical illness with a nominal extra premium.
Money Growth: Part of your money is invested in market funds, which can grow over time. This can help you build a strong fund for health requirements.
Fund Choices: You can choose where to put your money—high-risk (equity), low-risk (debt), or a mix (balanced).
Fund Switching: You can shift your money between funds based on market conditions.
Partial Withdrawals: After 5 years, you can take out some money in case of emergencies without closing the whole plan.
Top-Ups: You can add extra money whenever you have more savings. This helps you grow your fund faster.
Tax Benefits: Premiums you pay and the money you receive (under conditions) may qualify for tax benefits under Sections 80C and 10(10D).
These features make ULIPs helpful if you want to stay prepared for future medical needs.
How to Choose the Right ULIP for Health Benefits?
To choose the right ULIP for health-related needs, follow these simple steps:
Decide Your Budget: Know how much money you can pay regularly as a premium.
Set Your Health Goal: Think about what kind of medical costs you may need to cover in 5, 10, or 20 years.
Check Fund Options: Choose between equity (high returns, high risk), debt (safe, low returns), or a balanced mix.
Look at Past Fund Performance: Check how the funds have done in the last few years. This helps in making better choices.
Know the Lock-in Period: Usually, it’s 5 years. Make sure you’re okay with not using the money before this time.
Compare Charges: Some ULIPs have higher charges. Pick one with low costs so more of your money goes into your savings.
Check Top-Up Option: A good ULIP lets you add more money later to build a bigger health fund.
Read the Terms Carefully: Understand all the benefits, risks, and terms before you buy.
Choosing wisely ensures your ULIP helps when you or your family need it most.
Who Should Invest in a ULIPs for Health Benefits?
Taking a ULIP specifically to plan health benefits can benefit the following:
Young Earners: People starting their career can save early for future health issues.
Parents: Can build a fund for their children’s health needs.
Salaried Individuals: Helps in long-term planning for health and life cover.
People Close to Retirement: A good option to build a health fund for later years.
If you want protection and savings in one plan, this is a smart pick.
Advantages of ULIPs for Health Benefits
Here are some key benefits of a ULIP:
Combines life cover and savings in one plan, can add riders to cover critical illness
Helps plan for future health expenses
Partial withdrawal after 5 years for emergencies
Flexibility in choosing and switching funds
Top-up option to add more money
Tax benefits on premium and maturity value
Disadvantages of ULIPs for Health Benefits
Though ULIPs offer many benefits, there are a few things to be careful about:
Market-linked risks can affect returns
Returns are not fixed or guaranteed
Multiple charges can lower the net returns
Needs long-term commitment to see good growth
Always read the terms and understand the policy before buying.
Conclusion
A ULIP taken to manage health benefits is a good way to prepare for medical costs in the future. It gives your life protection and helps you grow your money over time. You can use this plan to build a health fund, add critical illness riders to cover financially if diagnosed with a critical disease, stay financially stable, and get tax benefits too. A ULIP:
Combines protection and savings
Can be used for health emergencies
Offers tax advantages under current rules
Helps you stay ready for unexpected health costs
If you want one plan for life cover and health planning, ULIPs can be useful. But read the terms and match them with your personal goals before you choose.
FAQs
Which is better, NPS or ULIP? NPS (National Pension System) is mainly used to save money for retirement. It helps you get monthly income after you stop working. ULIP (Unit Linked Insurance Plan) gives your life cover and also helps you build money for goals like health, children’s future, or emergencies. NPS is good if you only want retirement money. ULIP is better if you want both protection and flexible money use. So, it depends on what your goal is—retirement or more than that.
Is ULIP better than FD? FD (Fixed Deposit) offers guaranteed money after some years. FDs are safe and stable, but they grow slowly. ULIP has the potential for faster growth since it is market dependent. ULIP offers life insurance cover, health riders, and tax benefits which FDs do not provide. If you want no risk and by a fixed return stick with an FD but if you want to grow your money and have some protection, ULIP may be a good fit for you. Therefore, it depends on what level of risk you can accept, and your goal to achieve.
What are the advantages of ULIP? ULIP gives two big benefits in one plan—life cover and money savings. If something happens to you, your family gets a lump sum amount. Also, a part of your premium is used to grow money over time. You can choose where to put the money (low risk or high risk). ULIPs also offer tax savings. You can take out some money after 5 years if needed. You can even add more money anytime to build a bigger amount.
Does ULIP have death benefit?Yes, ULIPs offer a death benefit. If the policyholder dies during the policy period, the family will receive a specific amount of money called the "sum assured." This money is intended as a supplement to assist the family with expenses such as hospital fees, daily expenses, or schooling fees.  It provides comfort in the knowledge that the family will be taken care of during a difficult time. This is one major reason people prefer to purchase ULIPs, as they provide safety and assurance in difficult times.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility
full_pdf_text:
ULIP for Health Benefits: A Simple Way to Plan for Your Future
Medical Needs
Many people in India worry about the cost of medical treatment. Even if you have health insurance, some treatments like cancer or heart surgeries might not be fully covered. One way to stay prepared is by planning with a ULIP. Most people search for Health insurance ULIP, however, ULIP does not provide health insurance, it provides Life Insurance and money growth through market-linked investments.
With a ULIP, you can build a fund over time to help pay for future medical costs. It also gives your family financial protection and offers tax benefits, making it a smart way to plan for both health and life needs.
ULIP for Health Benefits
There are no Health insurance ULIPs however, you can still use ULIP to plan for future medical needs. While ULIPs mainly offer life cover and market-linked investment options, you can use the corpus built over time to support your health-related expenses. ULIPs focus more on long-term wealth creation, while some may offer optional riders for critical illness cover or accidental cover. Such riders help cover the health aspect along with the life coverage in the ULIP policy.
Features of ULIPs
ULIPs offer many useful features. They help you protect your family and also grow your money for health and other goals. Here are the main features and benefits:
● Life Insurance Cover: In the event of the life insured’s death, the sum assured (also called as death benefit) is paid to the nominee. This amount can help the family to manage their expenses like medical bills or daily expenses. You can also opt for health riders like a critical illness rider to get the treatment costs of a critical illness with a nominal extra premium. ● Money Growth: Part of your money is invested in market-linked funds, which can grow over time. Fund Choices: You can choose where to put your money—high-risk (equity), low-risk (debt), or a mix (balanced). ● Fund Switching: You can shift your money between funds based on market conditions.
● Partial Withdrawals: After 5 years, you can take out some money in case of emergencies without closing the whole plan. ● Top-Ups: You can add extra money whenever you have more savings. This helps you grow your fund faster. ● Tax Benefits: Premiums you pay and the money you receive (under conditions) may qualify for tax benefits under Sections 80C and 10(10D) of Income Tax Act 1961.
These features make ULIPs a valuable option for those seeking life insurance along with wealth creation through market-linked investments—also if you want to be financially prepared for future medical needs. Over time, the accumulated amount can serve as a medical fund to help cover major expenses such as surgeries, hospital bills, or long-term treatments.
How to Choose the Right ULIP ?
To choose the right ULIP for your goals , follow these simple steps:
● Decide Your Budget: Know how much money you can pay regularly as a premium. ● Set Your Goal: Think about what kind of costs you may need to cover in 5, 10, or 20 years. ● Check Fund Options: Choose between equity (high returns, high risk), debt (safe, low returns), or a balanced mix. ● Look at Past Fund Performance: Check how the funds have done in the last few years *. This helps in making better choices. ● Know the Lock-in Period: Usually, it’s 5 years. Make sure you’re okay with not using the money before this time. ● Compare Charges: Some ULIPs have higher charges. Pick one with low costs so more of your money goes into your savings. ● Check Top-Up Option: A good ULIP lets you add more money later to build a bigger fund. ● Read the Terms Carefully: Understand all the benefits, risks, and terms before you buy.
Choosing wisely ensures your ULIP helps when you or your family need it most.
Who Should Invest in a ULIPs ?
Taking a ULIP can benefit the following:
● Young Earners: People starting their careers. ● Parents: Can build a fund for their children’s needs. ● Salaried Individuals: Helps in long-term planning for health and life cover. ● People Close to Retirement: A good option to build a corpus for later years.
If you want protection and savings in one plan, this is a smart pick.
Conclusion
A ULIP taken to manage health benefits is a good way to prepare for medical costs in the future. It gives your life protection and helps you grow your money over time. You can use this plan to build a corpus to cover financially if diagnosed with a critical disease, stay financially stable, and get tax benefits too. A ULIP:
● Combines protection and savings ● Can be used for health emergencies ● Helps you stay ready for unexpected costs
If you want one plan for life cover and market-linked investment, the benefits/returns from which may help in health planning, ULIPs can be useful. But read the terms and match them with your personal goals before you choose.
FAQs
What are the advantages of ULIP? ULIP gives two big benefits in one plan—life cover and money savings. If something happens to you, your family gets a lump sum amount. Also, a part of your premium is used to grow money over time. You can choose where to put the money (low risk or high risk). ULIPs also offer tax savings. You can take out some money after 5 years if needed. You can even add more money anytime in the form of top-up facility to build a bigger amount.
Does ULIP have death benefit? Yes, ULIPs pay a sum assured (also called a death benefit) to the nominee in case of an unfortunate demise of the life assured. This money is intended as a supplement to assist the family with expenses such as hospital fees, daily expenses, or schooling fees. It provides comfort in the knowledge that the family will be taken care of during a difficult time.
Disclaimer: IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year. ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy *Past performance is not indicative of future performance BJAZ-WEB-EC-15683/25

changes.word_original: Though ULIPs offer many benefits, there are a few things to be careful about:
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Market-linked risks can affect returns
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Returns are not fixed or guaranteed
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Multiple charges can lower the net returns
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Needs long-term commitment to see good growth
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Always read the terms and understand the policy before buying.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: You can use this plan to build a health fund, add critical illness riders to cover financially if diagnosed with a critical disease, stay financially stable, and get tax benefits too.
changes.pdf_final: You can use this plan to build a corpus to cover financially if diagnosed with a critical disease, stay financially stable, and get tax benefits too.
changes.summary: Sentence rewritten in PDF

changes.word_original: Combines protection and savings
changes.pdf_final: ● Combines protection and savings ● Can be used for health emergencies ● Helps you stay ready for unexpected costs
changes.summary: Sentence rewritten in PDF

changes.word_original: Can be used for health emergencies
changes.pdf_final: If you want one plan for life cover and market-linked investment, the benefits/returns from which may help in health planning, ULIPs can be useful.
changes.summary: Sentence rewritten in PDF

changes.word_original: Offers tax advantages under current rules
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Helps you stay ready for unexpected health costs
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: If you want one plan for life cover and health planning, ULIPs can be useful.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Which is better, NPS or ULIP?
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: NPS (National Pension System) is mainly used to save money for retirement.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: It helps you get monthly income after you stop working.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: ULIP (Unit Linked Insurance Plan) gives your life cover and also helps you build money for goals like health, children’s future, or emergencies.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: NPS is good if you only want retirement money.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: ULIP is better if you want both protection and flexible money use.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: So, it depends on what your goal is—retirement or more than that.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Is ULIP better than FD?
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: FD (Fixed Deposit) offers guaranteed money after some years.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: FDs are safe and stable, but they grow slowly.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: ULIP has the potential for faster growth since it is market dependent.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: ULIP offers life insurance cover, health riders, and tax benefits which FDs do not provide.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: If you want no risk and by a fixed return stick with an FD but if you want to grow your money and have some protection, ULIP may be a good fit for you.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: Therefore, it depends on what level of risk you can accept, and your goal to achieve.
changes.summary: Sentence from Word removed in PDF

changes.word_original: You can even add more money anytime to build a bigger amount.
changes.pdf_final: You can even add more money anytime in the form of top-up facility to build a bigger amount.
changes.summary: Sentence rewritten in PDF

changes.word_original: Does ULIP have death benefit?Yes, ULIPs offer a death benefit.
changes.pdf_final: Does ULIP have death benefit?
changes.summary: Sentence rewritten in PDF

changes.word_original: If the policyholder dies during the policy period, the family will receive a specific amount of money called the "sum assured." This money is intended as a supplement to assist the family with expenses such as hospital fees, daily expenses, or schooling fees.
changes.pdf_final: Yes, ULIPs pay a sum assured (also called a death benefit) to the nominee in case of an unfortunate demise of the life assured.
changes.summary: Sentence rewritten in PDF

changes.pdf_final: This money is intended as a supplement to assist the family with expenses such as hospital fees, daily expenses, or schooling fees.
changes.summary: New sentence added in PDF

changes.word_original: This is one major reason people prefer to purchase ULIPs, as they provide safety and assurance in difficult times.
